title: mrpipeline – Package Developer Guide for Claude Code
text: mrpipeline R package Mendelian randomisation (MR) colocalization analysis, currently focused proteomic GWAS data. However, plan integrate eQTL, scQTL GWAS data. Core exported functions: run_mr() – Cis-MR, genome-wide MR, manual-instrument MR sensitivity methods run_coloc() – Colocalization (coloc.abf, SuSiE, coloc.signals, colocPropTest) format_pqtl_decode() – Format deCODE proteomics GWAS TwoSampleMR exposure format format_pqtl_ukbppp() – Format UKB-PPP pQTL data TwoSampleMR exposure format format_single_cell_onek1k() – Format OneK1K single-cell eQTL data Internal helpers (exported, R/helpers.R): harmonise_and_filter(), compute_ld_matrix(), clump_instruments(), align_to_ld_matrix(), eaf_to_maf(), resolve_sample_size() S3 classes: mr_result (run_mr()), coloc_result (run_coloc())

title: mrpipeline – Package Developer Guide for Claude Code
code: devtools::load_all()      # load package for interactive development devtools::document()      # regenerate NAMESPACE and man/ from roxygen2 devtools::check()         # R CMD Check (must pass: 0 errors, 0 warnings) devtools::test()          # run testthat tests pkgdown::build_site()     # build documentation site (must succeed) air format .              # auto-format R code (run before every commit) lintr::lint_package()     # style/lint checks (run before every commit)

title: mrpipeline – Package Developer Guide for Claude Code
text: ASCII : Never use non-ASCII characters anywhere .R .Rd files – R CMD check warns CI fail. Common offenders: - Em dash (Unicode U+2014) – write -- instead - Box-drawing horizontal (Unicode U+2500, used RStudio section headers) – write - instead - Right arrow (Unicode U+2192) – write -> instead - Unicode punctuation symbols Section dividers code comments must use plain hyphens: # -- Section name --. Messages / warnings / errors: Use cli package exclusively. Pipe: Native |> . Never %>%. String operations: Use stringr functions. Namespace: Prefer pkg::fn() @importFrom. Internal helpers: Tag @keywords internal; use @export. Documentation: Use roxygen2 functions. Vignettes (mrpipeline-user-guide.Rmd, mrpipeline-developer-guide.Rmd) living documents – update commit API change: - adding/changing/removing function parameter: update roxygen, user guide (usage examples), developer guide (architecture/internals) - adding/removing internal helpers: update developer guide’s helpers section - modifying S3 class structure: update developer guide’s S3 classes section verbose argument: Planned future feature. added, gate cli::cli_inform() calls behind .
code: cli::cli_inform("Loading {protein} data...")   # informational cli::cli_warn("Only {n} SNPs retained.")       # warning cli::cli_abort("bfile is required for LD-corrected MR.")  # error stringr::str_remove(x, "_.*")      # not gsub("_.*", "", x) stringr::str_detect(x, "^chr")     # not grepl("^chr", x) stringr::str_starts(x, "rs")       # not startsWith(x, "rs")

text: Feature branches created dev merged back dev via PR. feature corresponding GitHub issue. Branch naming: phase-N/short-description (e.g. phase-1/shared-helpers).

title: mrpipeline Developer Guide
text: Every run_mr() call returns mr_result object — even instruments survive filtering. status field indicates success failure: "success" — analysis completed normally "no_instruments" — instruments survived filtering/clumping/exclusion "no_harmonised_variants" — harmonisation removed variants status_reason field provides human-readable explanation (e.g. "significant instruments cis region 'PCSK9'"). print.mr_result() summary.mr_result() display status information non-success results.

title: mrpipeline Developer Guide
text: Every run_coloc() call returns coloc_result object — even analysis proceed (e.g. SNPs region). object contains: coloc_abf — output coloc::coloc.abf(), NULL coloc_susie — output coloc::coloc.susie(), NULL coloc_signals — output coloc::coloc.signals(), NULL coloc_prop_test — output colocPropTest::coloc.prop.test(), NULL n_snps — integer, number SNPs used analysis harmonised_data — data frame harmonised data methods_skipped — named character vector (method name → reason skipped) params — list input parameters status — one "success", "no_snps_in_region", "too_few_snps", "no_harmonised_variants" status_reason — human-readable explanation status != "success" print.coloc_result() shows one-line summary: N SNPs, ABF PP.H4, SuSiE max PP.H4 credible set count (available). summary.coloc_result() shows full details: posterior probabilities (H0–H4), SuSiE credible set pairs, signals hits, skipped methods.

title: mrpipeline Developer Guide
text: S3 classes plot() methods defined R/plot.R: "scatter" (default): TwoSampleMR::mr_scatter_plot() "forest": TwoSampleMR::mr_forest_plot() "funnel": TwoSampleMR::mr_funnel_plot() "pp_bar" (default): bar chart ABF posterior probabilities (H0–H4) "regional": side--side regional association plots (-log10(p) vs position) exposure outcome methods return NULL invisibly non-success results informative message.

title: mrpipeline Developer Guide
text: Defined R/get_gene_coords.R. Queries Ensembl via biomaRt gene coordinates. Key design decisions: Uses biomaRt::useEnsembl() host = "grch37.ensembl.org" GRCh37 default host GRCh38 Filters standard chromosomes (1–22, X, Y) Deduplicates gene + chromosome (widest range), preferring autosomes Warns genes found Ensembl

title: mrpipeline Developer Guide
text: helpers live R/helpers.R, tagged @keywords internal, exported.

title: mrpipeline Developer Guide
text: Resolves PLINK resource limits (threads, memory) R options environment variables. Checks getOption("mrpipeline.plink_{param}") first, falls back MRPIPELINE_PLINK_{PARAM} environment variable. Returns NULL neither set (PLINK auto-detects). Used default value plink_threads plink_memory run_mr() run_coloc().

title: mrpipeline Developer Guide
text: Always use cli package. Never use message(), warning(), stop() directly. verbose parameter planned future feature. implemented, cli::cli_inform() calls gated behind .
code: cli::cli_inform("Processing {protein}...")      # informational (goes to stderr) cli::cli_warn("Only {n} SNP(s) available.")     # warning cli::cli_abort("bfile is required for coloc.")  # error (stops execution)

title: mrpipeline Developer Guide
text: Use native |> pipe exclusively. Never use %>% magrittr.

title: mrpipeline Developer Guide
text: Prefer stringr functions base R equivalents:
code: # Good stringr::str_remove(x, "_.*") stringr::str_detect(x, "^chr") stringr::str_replace(x, "chr", "")  # Avoid gsub("_.*", "", x) grepl("^chr", x) sub("chr", "", x)

title: mrpipeline Developer Guide
text: Prefer pkg::fn() @importFrom. add @importFrom entries function called frequently hot path.

title: mrpipeline Developer Guide
text: exported functions: @param, @return, @examples, @seealso, @family Internal helpers: @keywords internal ; @export modifying exported function, update roxygen docs vignette commit

title: mrpipeline Developer Guide
text: package ships several bundled datasets (defined R/data.R) minimal LD reference panel. SNP-level datasets overlap LD panel, integration tests can run without external downloads.

title: mrpipeline Developer Guide
text: inst/extdata/ld_ref.{bed,bim,fam} — synthetic PLINK binary fileset 50 biallelic SNPs CD40 chr20 cis region (positions 44646911–44858502) 100 individuals. Allele frequencies drawn real cd40_sumstats data, LD structure random allele frequencies realistic. Total size ~4 KB. Access bfile prefix tests: Guard integration tests need plink:
code: bfile <- sub("\\.bed$", "", system.file("extdata", "ld_ref.bed", package = "mrpipeline")) skip_if_not(file.exists(paste0(bfile, ".bed")), "LD reference panel not available")

title: mrpipeline Developer Guide
text: opening pull request: air format . — auto-format R files (CLI tool) lintr::lint_package() — fix lint warnings pkgdown::build_site() — confirm site builds without errors devtools::check() — must produce 0 errors, 0 warnings devtools::test() — tests must pass

title: mrpipeline User Guide
text: Use get_gene_coords() programmatically retrieve gene coordinates Ensembl via biomaRt. useful defining cis regions run_mr() run_coloc() without hard-coding coordinates. returned tibble can feed directly run_mr() run_coloc():
code: # GRCh38 (default) coords <- get_gene_coords(c("CD40", "APOE")) coords #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   hgnc_symbol chromosome     start       end #>   <chr>       <chr>          <int>     <int> #> 1 APOE        19          44905791  44909393 #> 2 CD40        20          44746911  44758502  # GRCh37 coords_37 <- get_gene_coords("CD40", build = "grch37") cd40 <- get_gene_coords("CD40", build = "grch38")  mr_res <- run_mr(   exposure = exposure_data,   exposure_id = "CD40",   outcome = outcome_data,   outcome_id = "SjD",   instrument_region = list(     chromosome = cd40$chromosome,     start = cd40$start,     end = cd40$end   ) )

title: mrpipeline User Guide
text: running many parallel jobs (e.g. HPC cluster), PLINK’s default behaviour auto-detecting available threads memory can cause problems — worker may try reserve half node’s RAM. Use plink_threads plink_memory cap resource usage per call. can set per-call, via R options, via environment variables: Priority order: explicit argument > R option > environment variable > PLINK auto-detect (NULL). parameters available run_coloc().
code: # Per-call (highest priority) result <- run_mr(  exposure = exposure_data, exposure_id = "CD40",  outcome = outcome_data, outcome_id = "SjD",  bfile = "/path/to/ld_ref",  plink_threads = 1,  plink_memory = 2000 )  # Via R options (apply to all subsequent calls in the session) options(  mrpipeline.plink_threads = 1,  mrpipeline.plink_memory = 2000 )  # Via environment variables (e.g. in .Renviron or a job script) # MRPIPELINE_PLINK_THREADS=1 # MRPIPELINE_PLINK_MEMORY=2000

title: mrpipeline User Guide
text: Use exclude_regions argument remove instruments falling specific genomic regions. commonly used exclude MHC region chromosome 6, can introduce spurious associations due complex LD structure. Supply data frame columns chr, start, end: can exclude multiple regions stacking rows. example, studying age-related macular degeneration (AMD), might exclude CFH ARMS2/HTRA1 loci:
code: # Exclude MHC region (GRCh37 coordinates: chr6:28,477,797–33,448,354) mhc_grch37 <- data.frame(chr = "6", start = 28477797, end = 33448354)  result <- run_mr(   exposure = exposure_data,   exposure_id = "PCSK9",   outcome = outcome_data,   outcome_id = "CHD",   exclude_regions = mhc_grch37 )  # GRCh38 coordinates: chr6:28,510,120–33,480,577 mhc_grch38 <- data.frame(chr = "6", start = 28510120, end = 33480577) amd_exclusions <- data.frame(   chr = c("1", "10"),   start = c(196621008, 124214077),   end = c(196716634, 124274424) )  result <- run_mr(   exposure = exposure_data,   exposure_id = "CFH",   outcome = outcome_data,   outcome_id = "AMD",   exclude_regions = amd_exclusions )

title: mrpipeline User Guide
text: can bypass automatic instrument selection supplying character vector rsIDs instruments argument: default, instruments missing exposure data produce warning analysis continues available SNPs. Set instruments_strict = TRUE error instead:
code: result <- run_mr(   exposure = exposure_data,   exposure_id = "PCSK9",   outcome = outcome_data,   outcome_id = "CHD",   instruments = c("rs11591147", "rs2479409", "rs11583680") ) result <- run_mr(   exposure = exposure_data,   exposure_id = "PCSK9",   outcome = outcome_data,   outcome_id = "CHD",   instruments = c("rs11591147", "rs2479409"),   instruments_strict = TRUE )

title: mrpipeline User Guide
text: Use print() one-line summary summary() full details:
code: mr_res summary(mr_res)

title: mrpipeline User Guide
text: plot() produces diagnostic plots using TwoSampleMR plotting functions (requires ggplot2):
code: plot(mr_res, type = "scatter") # scatter plot (default) plot(mr_res, type = "forest")  # forest plot plot(mr_res, type = "funnel")  # funnel plot

title: mrpipeline User Guide
text: simplest colocalization test uses Approximate Bayes Factors (ABF). Supply pre-formatted exposure data, raw outcome data, gene region, LD reference panel:
code: result <- run_coloc(   exposure = formatted_exposure,   outcome = outcome_data,   gene_chr = 20,   gene_start = 44746911,   gene_end = 44758502,   exposure_n = 35559,   outcome_n = 400000,   bfile = "/path/to/ld_reference",   methods = "abf" ) print(result) summary(result)  # Access posterior probabilities directly result$coloc_abf$summary

title: mrpipeline User Guide
text: multi-signal colocalization, include "susie" "signals" methods argument. SuSiE fine-maps trait independently, coloc.susie() coloc.signals() test colocalization across pairs credible sets:
code: result <- run_coloc(   exposure = formatted_exposure,   outcome = outcome_data,   gene_chr = 20,   gene_start = 44746911,   gene_end = 44758502,   exposure_n = 35559,   outcome_n = 400000,   bfile = "/path/to/ld_reference",   methods = c("abf", "susie", "signals") ) summary(result)

title: mrpipeline User Guide
text: case-control outcomes, set outcome_type = "cc" provide proportion cases via outcome_s:
code: result <- run_coloc(   exposure = formatted_exposure,   outcome = outcome_data,   gene_chr = 20,   gene_start = 44746911,   gene_end = 44758502,   exposure_n = 35559,   outcome_n = 400000,   outcome_type = "cc",   outcome_s = 0.3,   bfile = "/path/to/ld_reference",   methods = c("abf", "susie", "signals") )

title: mrpipeline User Guide
text: plot() coloc_result objects supports two plot types (requires ggplot2):
code: plot(coloc_res, type = "pp_bar")    # bar chart of ABF posterior probabilities (default) plot(coloc_res, type = "regional")  # regional association plots

title: Getting Started with mrpipeline
text: Install GitHub:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("BZuckerman97/mrpipeline")

title: Getting Started with mrpipeline
code: library(mrpipeline)

title: Getting Started with mrpipeline
text: mrpipeline ships bundled test datasets CD40 protein Sjogren’s disease. Use explore package without external data. mr_result object stores full results table, harmonised instruments, F-statistics, Steiger filtering output, skipped methods — accessible via mr_res$results, mr_res$instruments, etc.
code: # Bundled datasets: cd40_exposure (formatted exposure), sjogren_outcome (outcome) bfile <- system.file("extdata", "ld_ref", package = "mrpipeline")  # Run cis-MR mr_res <- run_mr(   exposure = cd40_exposure,   exposure_id = "CD40",   outcome = sjogren_outcome,    outcome_id = "SjD",   instrument_region = list(chromosome = "20", start = 44746911, end = 44758502),   bfile = bfile,   methods = c("ivw", "egger", "weighted_median") )  # Inspect results mr_res summary(mr_res)  # Plot (requires ggplot2) plot(mr_res, type = "scatter") plot(mr_res, type = "forest")

title: Getting Started with mrpipeline
code: coloc_res <- run_coloc(   exposure = cd40_exposure,   outcome = sjogren_outcome,   gene_chr = "20",   gene_start = 44746911,   gene_end = 44758502,   bfile = bfile,   methods = "abf" )  coloc_res summary(coloc_res) plot(coloc_res, type = "pp_bar")

title: Getting Started with mrpipeline
text: Look genomic coordinates HGNC gene symbols via Ensembl (requires biomaRt package): coordinates can passed directly run_mr() run_coloc() via instrument_region gene_* arguments.
code: coords <- get_gene_coords(c("CD40", "APOE"), build = "grch38") coords

title: Getting Started with mrpipeline
text: mrpipeline includes formatters common proteomic GWAS sources: format_pqtl_decode() — deCODE genetics format_pqtl_ukbppp() — UKB-PPP (Olink) format_single_cell_onek1k() — OneK1K single-cell eQTL returns data formatted TwoSampleMR, ready pass run_mr().

title: Authors and Citation
text: Benjamin Zuckerman. Author, maintainer. Alasdair Warwick. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Zuckerman B, Warwick (2026). mrpipeline: Functions set pipeline performing Mendelian randomization. R package version 0.4.1.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mrpipeline: Functions to set up a pipeline for performing Mendelian randomization},   author = {Benjamin Zuckerman and Alasdair Warwick},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.4.1}, }

what: Functions to set up a pipeline for performing Mendelian randomization
title: Functions to set up a pipeline for performing Mendelian randomization
text: mrpipeline provides streamlined interface Mendelian randomisation (MR) colocalization analysis, focus proteomic GWAS data (deCODE, UKB-PPP). wraps TwoSampleMR, coloc, MendelianRandomization consistent workflow S3 result objects built-sensitivity analyses.

title: Functions to set up a pipeline for performing Mendelian randomization
text: Install GitHub:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("BZuckerman97/mrpipeline")

title: Functions to set up a pipeline for performing Mendelian randomization
code: library(mrpipeline)  # Format exposure data (e.g. UKB-PPP pQTL) exposure <- format_pqtl_ukbppp(cd40_sumstats, exposure_id = "CD40")  # Look up gene coordinates coords <- get_gene_coords("CD40", build = "grch38")  # Run cis-MR mr_res <- run_mr(   exposure = exposure,   exposure_id = "CD40",   outcome = sjogren_outcome,   outcome_id = "SjD",   instrument_region = list(     chromosome = coords$chromosome,     start = coords$start,     end = coords$end   ) ) mr_res summary(mr_res)  # Run colocalization coloc_res <- run_coloc(   exposure = exposure,   outcome = sjogren_outcome,   gene_chr = coords$chromosome,   gene_start = coords$start,   gene_end = coords$end,   bfile = "path/to/ld_reference" ) coloc_res summary(coloc_res)

title: Functions to set up a pipeline for performing Mendelian randomization
text: vignette("mrpipeline-user-guide") — end--end usage examples vignette("mrpipeline-developer-guide") — architecture internals

what: Align harmonised data to an LD matrix — align_to_ld_matrix
title: Align harmonised data to an LD matrix — align_to_ld_matrix
text: Subsets harmonised data frame LD matrix shared SNPs, reorders match.

title: Align harmonised data to an LD matrix — align_to_ld_matrix
code: align_to_ld_matrix(harmonised_data, ld_matrix)

title: Align harmonised data to an LD matrix — align_to_ld_matrix
text: harmonised_data Data frame SNP column. ld_matrix Square matrix rsID row/column names.

title: Align harmonised data to an LD matrix — align_to_ld_matrix
text: named list elements: data: subset reordered data frame ld_matrix: subset reordered matrix

what: Clump instruments using LD reference — clump_instruments
title: Clump instruments using LD reference — clump_instruments
text: Computes pseudo p-values (ensure correct ranking PLINK) performs LD clumping via ieugwasr::ld_clump().

title: Clump instruments using LD reference — clump_instruments
code: clump_instruments(   dat,   rsq_thresh,   clump_kb = 10000,   bfile = NULL,   plink_bin = NULL,   pop = "EUR",   plink_threads = NULL,   plink_memory = NULL )

title: Clump instruments using LD reference — clump_instruments
text: dat Data frame columns rsid, pval, id, optionally beta se (used pseudo p-value computation). beta se absent, pval used directly. rsq_thresh R-squared clumping threshold. clump_kb Clumping window kb. Default 10000. bfile Path PLINK bfile prefix. NULL, uses API clumping. plink_bin Path PLINK binary. NULL, auto-detected. pop Population API clumping. Default "EUR".

title: Clump instruments using LD reference — clump_instruments
text: Data frame clumped variants (output ieugwasr::ld_clump()).

title: Clump instruments using LD reference — clump_instruments
text: Pseudo p-values generated : Computing z-scores: beta / se Computing precise -log10(p) via Rmpfr::pnorm() Ranking SNPs -log10(p) descending Assigning evenly spaced pseudo p-values 1e-100 0.9

what: Compute LD correlation matrix from a local reference panel — compute_ld_matrix
title: Compute LD correlation matrix from a local reference panel — compute_ld_matrix
text: Calls ieugwasr::ld_matrix() strips allele suffixes (e.g. rs123_A_G -> rs123) row column names.

title: Compute LD correlation matrix from a local reference panel — compute_ld_matrix
code: compute_ld_matrix(   snps,   bfile,   plink_bin = NULL,   plink_threads = NULL,   plink_memory = NULL )

title: Compute LD correlation matrix from a local reference panel — compute_ld_matrix
text: snps Character vector rsIDs. bfile Path PLINK bfile prefix (without .bed/.bim/.fam). plink_bin Path PLINK binary. NULL, auto-detected via genetics.binaRies::get_plink_binary(). plink_threads Number threads PLINK. NULL = auto-detect. plink_memory Memory (MB) PLINK. NULL = auto-detect.

title: Compute LD correlation matrix from a local reference panel — compute_ld_matrix
text: square correlation matrix rsID-row/column names.

title: DECODE_PQTL_FILE_NAME — decode_pqtl_file_name
text: DECODE_PQTL_FILE_NAME

code: decode_pqtl_file_name(unique_id, decode_linker_file, decode_dir)

title: DECODE_PQTL_FILE_NAME — decode_pqtl_file_name
text: unique_id String, unique sequence associated protein assayed decode_linker_file String Dataframe, file path file containing deCDOE metadata decode_dir String, file path directory deCODE summary statistics present

title: DECODE_PQTL_FILE_NAME — decode_pqtl_file_name
text: list file paths deCODE proteomic summary statistics

title: DECODE_PQTL_FILE_NAME — decode_pqtl_file_name
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Assuming you have a linker file and the deCODE data directory linker_file <- "path/to/your/decode_linker.csv" decode_data_dir <- "path/to/decode_summary_stats/" # Get the first unique ID from the linker for demonstration unique_protein_id <- data.table::fread(linker_file)$seqID[1] file_paths <- decode_pqtl_file_name(unique_id = unique_protein_id,                                   decode_linker_file = linker_file,                                   decode_dir = decode_data_dir) print(file_paths) } # }

what: Format GWAS summary statistics to a standard schema — format_gwas
title: Format GWAS summary statistics to a standard schema — format_gwas
text: Normalises column names GWAS summary statistics file canonical schema expected run_mr() run_coloc(). Handles datasets rsIDs absent looking PLINK bim file, supports extracting chromosome position compound marker ID columns (e.g. SCALLOP MarkerName format: "CHR:POS:A1_A2").

title: Format GWAS summary statistics to a standard schema — format_gwas
code: format_gwas(   path,   phenotype_id,   type = c("outcome", "exposure"),   col_map = NULL,   bim_path = NULL,   marker_col = NULL,   marker_sep = ":",   log10_pval = FALSE,   flip_beta = FALSE,   n = NULL )

title: Format GWAS summary statistics to a standard schema — format_gwas
text: path Character file path (.tsv, .tsv.gz, .txt.gz, etc. – data.table::fread() auto-detects compression) pre-loaded data frame. phenotype_id Character. Trait / phenotype identifier (e.g. "IL-18", "CAD"). Stored phenotype output column. type "outcome" (default) "exposure". "outcome" returns normalised data frame ready run_mr()'s outcome argument. "exposure" additionally calls TwoSampleMR::format_data() returns TwoSampleMR-formatted data exposure. column suffixes. col_map Named list extra column-name aliases, e.g. list(pval = "PVALUE", n = "SampleSize"). needed column names already covered built-alias table (see Column normalisation section). User entries checked built-list. bim_path Character. Path PLINK bfile prefix (without .bim) used recover rsIDs data lacks . Required whenever rsids column absent. marker_col Character. Name compound marker ID column "CHR<sep>POS<sep>..." format (e.g. "MarkerName" SCALLOP files). supplied, chr pos parsed column. marker_sep Character. Field separator used marker_col. Default ":". log10_pval Logical. TRUE, p-value column -log10 scale back-transformed via 10^-x. Default FALSE. flip_beta Logical. TRUE, multiplies beta -1 – use source file encodes inverse direction intended exposure (e.g. modelling NLRP3 activation rather suppression). Default FALSE. n Integer. Explicit sample size. Added n column sample-size column already present data.

title: Format GWAS summary statistics to a standard schema — format_gwas
text: type = "outcome": data frame columns rsids, chr, pos, beta, se, eaf, pval, n, effect_allele, other_allele, phenotype (plus extra columns source file). type = "exposure": TwoSampleMR-formatted data frame (output TwoSampleMR::format_data()) exposure.-suffixed columns, suitable run_mr()'s exposure argument.

title: Format GWAS summary statistics to a standard schema — format_gwas
text: function renames source columns fixed canonical schema checking built-table known aliases target column: Supply col_map dataset uses column name appear table – example, p-value column called "PVALUE", add col_map = list(pval = "PVALUE"). Inspect names() loaded data check. User-supplied aliases checked built-list, take precedence event ambiguity.

title: Format GWAS summary statistics to a standard schema — format_gwas
text: rsids absent (NA) column normalisation, bim_path supplied, function inner-joins data PLINK bim file chromosome position recover rsIDs. Rows without bim match dropped – absent reference panel used LD-based analyses. message reports many SNPs retained.

title: Format GWAS summary statistics to a standard schema — format_gwas
text: Set marker_col name compound marker ID column whose values form "CHR<sep>POS<sep>..." (e.g. SCALLOP "MarkerName"). chr pos extracted first two fields. step runs rsID lookup extracted coordinates available bim join.

title: Format GWAS summary statistics to a standard schema — format_gwas
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Outcome GWAS whose columns are already in the built-in alias table cad <- format_gwas(   path         = "genomics_data/outcome_GWAS/CAD/cad_gwas.tsv.gz",   phenotype_id = "CAD" )  # SCALLOP outcome: no rsIDs, chr+pos embedded in MarkerName column scallop_il6 <- format_gwas(   path         = "genomics_data/outcome_GWAS/SCALLOP/CVD1_IL6.tsv.gz",   phenotype_id = "IL6",   marker_col   = "MarkerName",   bim_path     = "LD_ref/g1000_eur" )  # Dataset with a non-standard p-value column not in the alias table ebi_il18 <- format_gwas(   path         = "genomics_data/outcome_GWAS/EBI/GCST90428399.tsv.gz",   phenotype_id = "IL-18",   col_map      = list(pval = "PVALUE") )  # Exposure GWAS -- flip beta to model NLRP3 activation not suppression exposure <- format_gwas(   path         = "NLRP3/Output/NLRP3_CRP_IVs_300kb.tsv",   phenotype_id = "NLRP3",   type         = "exposure",   flip_beta    = TRUE ) } # }

what: Look up gene coordinates from Ensembl via biomaRt — get_gene_coords

title: Look up gene coordinates from Ensembl via biomaRt — get_gene_coords
text: tibble columns hgnc_symbol, chromosome, start, end. Genes found Ensembl dropped warning.

title: Look up gene coordinates from Ensembl via biomaRt — get_gene_coords
code: get_gene_coords("CD40") #> Ensembl site unresponsive, trying asia mirror #> Ensembl site unresponsive, trying useast mirror #> Error in .chooseEnsemblMirror(mirror = mirror, http_config = http_config): Unable to query any Ensembl site get_gene_coords(c("CD40", "APOE"), build = "grch37") #> Error in req_perform(request): HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout.

title: Create a coloc_result object — new_coloc_result
text: coloc_abf Output coloc::coloc.abf(), NULL. coloc_susie Output coloc::coloc.susie(), NULL. coloc_signals Output coloc::coloc.signals(), NULL. coloc_prop_test Output colocPropTest::coloc.prop.test(), NULL. n_snps Integer. Number SNPs used analysis. harmonised_data Data frame harmonised data used analysis. methods_skipped Named character vector: names method names, values reasons skipping. params List input parameters run_coloc(). status Character. One "success", "no_snps_in_region", "too_few_snps", "no_harmonised_variants". Default "success". status_reason Character NULL. Human-readable explanation status != "success". timing Named numeric vector elapsed times (seconds) major step inside run_coloc(). Empty default.

title: Create an mr_result object — new_mr_result
text: results Data frame columns: exposure, outcome, method, nsnp, b, se, pval. instruments Data frame harmonised (clumped) instrument data. f_stats List elements per_snp (numeric vector), mean (numeric scalar), min (numeric scalar). steiger Output TwoSampleMR::steiger_filtering(), NULL. methods_skipped Named character vector: names method names, values reasons skipping. ld_matrix LD correlation matrix ld_correct = TRUE, NULL. params List input parameters run_mr(). status Character. One "success", "no_instruments", "no_harmonised_variants". Default "success". status_reason Character NULL. Human-readable explanation status != "success". timing Named numeric vector elapsed times (seconds) major step inside run_mr(). Empty default.

title: Perform colocalization analysis — run_coloc
text: Runs colocalization pre-formatted exposure raw outcome dataset within specified gene region. Supports coloc.abf, SuSiE-based coloc (coloc.susie), coloc.signals, colocPropTest. Returns coloc_result S3 object.

title: Perform colocalization analysis — run_coloc
code: run_coloc(   exposure,   outcome,   gene_chr,   gene_start,   gene_end,   coloc_window = 10000L,   exposure_n = NULL,   outcome_n = NULL,   exposure_type = c("quant", "cc"),   outcome_type = c("quant", "cc"),   exposure_s = NULL,   outcome_s = NULL,   exposure_sdY = 1,   outcome_sdY = 1,   bfile,   plink_bin = NULL,   methods = c("abf", "susie", "signals"),   p1 = 1e-04,   p2 = 1e-04,   p12 = 1e-05,   plink_threads = plink_option("threads"),   plink_memory = plink_option("memory"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Perform colocalization analysis — run_coloc
text: exposure Data frame formatted exposure data (output TwoSampleMR::format_data() format_pqtl_*() functions). outcome Data frame outcome summary statistics standardised columns: rsids, chr, pos, beta, se, eaf, pval, n, effect_allele, other_allele. gene_chr Character integer. Chromosome gene/region. gene_start Integer. Start position (bp) gene/region. gene_end Integer. End position (bp) gene/region. coloc_window Integer. Window base pairs extend around gene region. Default 10000L (10 kb). exposure_n Integer. Exposure sample size. NULL, inferred samplesize.exposure column. outcome_n Integer. Outcome sample size. NULL, inferred n column outcome. exposure_type Character. Type exposure trait: "quant" "cc". Default "quant". outcome_type Character. Type outcome trait: "quant" "cc". Default "quant". exposure_s Numeric. Proportion cases case-control exposure. Required exposure_type = "cc". outcome_s Numeric. Proportion cases case-control outcome. Required outcome_type = "cc". exposure_sdY Numeric. Standard deviation exposure trait (quantitative traits). Default 1. outcome_sdY Numeric. Standard deviation outcome trait (quantitative traits). Default 1. bfile Character. Path PLINK bfile prefix (without .bed/.bim/.fam) LD reference. Required. plink_bin Character. Path PLINK binary. Auto-detected NULL. methods Character vector colocalization methods run. Options: "abf", "susie", "signals", "prop_test". Default c("abf", "susie", "signals"). p1 Numeric. Prior probability SNP associated trait 1. Default 1e-4. p2 Numeric. Prior probability SNP associated trait 2. Default 1e-4. p12 Numeric. Prior probability SNP associated traits. Default 1e-5. plink_threads Integer. Number threads PLINK. NULL (default) lets PLINK auto-detect. Read getOption("mrpipeline.plink_threads") MRPIPELINE_PLINK_THREADS environment variable via plink_option(). plink_memory Integer. Memory limit MB PLINK. NULL (default) lets PLINK auto-detect. Read getOption("mrpipeline.plink_memory") MRPIPELINE_PLINK_MEMORY environment variable via plink_option(). verbose Logical. TRUE, emit informational messages via cli::cli_inform(). Warnings errors always emitted regardless. Default TRUE.

title: Perform colocalization analysis — run_coloc
text: coloc_result object. Check result$status "success" vs failure reasons. $timing field contains named numeric vector elapsed seconds major step.

title: Perform colocalization analysis — run_coloc
text: "abf" – Approximate Bayes Factor colocalization via coloc::coloc.abf() "susie" – SuSiE fine-mapping + colocalization via coloc::runsusie() coloc::coloc.susie() "signals" – Multi-signal colocalization via coloc::coloc.signals(). Requires "susie" run successfully. "prop_test" – Proportionality test via colocPropTest::coloc.prop.test(). Requires "signals" run successfully colocPropTest package installed.

title: Perform colocalization analysis — run_coloc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- run_coloc(   exposure = formatted_exposure,   outcome = outcome_data,   gene_chr = 20, gene_start = 44746911, gene_end = 44758502,   bfile = "/path/to/ld_reference",   methods = "abf" ) print(result) summary(result) } # }

title: Perform Mendelian randomisation analysis — run_mr
text: Runs MR automatic instrument selection (cis-MR, genome-wide, manual) optional sensitivity analyses. Returns mr_result S3 object.

title: Perform Mendelian randomisation analysis — run_mr
code: run_mr(   exposure,   exposure_id,   outcome,   outcome_id,   instrument_region = NULL,   window = 100000L,   pval_thresh = 5e-08,   rsq_thresh = 0.001,   bfile = NULL,   plink_bin = NULL,   pop = "EUR",   instruments = NULL,   instruments_strict = FALSE,   exclude_regions = NULL,   methods = c("ivw", "egger", "weighted_median", "presso", "conmix", "steiger"),   ld_correct = FALSE,   exposure_n = NULL,   presso_n_dist = 1000,   plink_threads = plink_option("threads"),   plink_memory = plink_option("memory"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Perform Mendelian randomisation analysis — run_mr
text: exposure Data frame formatted exposure data (output TwoSampleMR::format_data() format_pqtl_*() functions). exposure_id Character. Identifier exposure (e.g. protein name). outcome Data frame outcome summary statistics standardised columns: rsids, chr, pos, beta, se, eaf, pval, n, effect_allele, other_allele. Formatted internally via TwoSampleMR::format_data(). outcome_id Character. Identifier outcome (e.g. disease name). instrument_region List elements chromosome, start, end defining cis region. NULL genome-wide manual mode. window Integer. Window (bp) extend either side instrument_region. Default 100000L. pval_thresh Numeric. P-value threshold instrument selection. Default 5e-8. rsq_thresh Numeric. R-squared clumping threshold. Default 0.001. bfile Character. Path PLINK bfile prefix local LD operations. Required ld_correct = TRUE. plink_bin Character. Path PLINK binary. Auto-detected NULL. pop Character. Population API-based LD clumping. Default "EUR". instruments Character vector rsIDs manual instrument mode, NULL. instruments_strict Logical. TRUE, error manual instruments missing exposure data. FALSE, warn. Default FALSE. exclude_regions Data frame columns chr, start, end defining genomic regions exclude instruments , NULL. example, exclude MHC region: data.frame(chr = "6", start = 26e6, end = 34e6). methods Character vector MR methods run. Options: "ivw", "egger", "weighted_median", "presso", "conmix", "steiger". ld_correct Logical. Use LD-corrected IVW/Egger via MendelianRandomization package. Requires bfile. Default FALSE. exposure_n Numeric. Exposure sample size. NULL, inferred samplesize.exposure column. presso_n_dist Integer. Number distributions MR-PRESSO. Default 1000. plink_threads Integer. Number threads PLINK. NULL (default) lets PLINK auto-detect. Read getOption("mrpipeline.plink_threads") MRPIPELINE_PLINK_THREADS environment variable via plink_option(). plink_memory Integer. Memory limit MB PLINK. NULL (default) lets PLINK auto-detect. Read getOption("mrpipeline.plink_memory") MRPIPELINE_PLINK_MEMORY environment variable via plink_option(). verbose Logical. TRUE, emit informational messages via cli::cli_inform(). Warnings errors always emitted regardless. Default TRUE.

title: Perform Mendelian randomisation analysis — run_mr
text: mr_result object. Check result$status "success" vs failure reasons. $timing field contains named numeric vector elapsed seconds major step.

title: Perform Mendelian randomisation analysis — run_mr
text: Exactly one three modes used, determined combination instruments instrument_region: Cis-MR (instrument_region provided, instruments = NULL): filters exposure cis region defined instrument_region +/- window, applies pval_thresh, LD-clumps. Genome-wide (instrument_region = NULL, instruments = NULL): filters exposure pval_thresh , LD-clumps. Manual (instruments provided): uses supplied rsIDs directly. instruments_strict controls whether missing IDs error warning.

title: Perform Mendelian randomisation analysis — run_mr
text: Methods dispatched based number instruments clumping: 1 SNP: Wald ratio 2 SNPs: IVW (+ ConMix/Steiger requested); Egger/weighted_median/PRESSO skipped 3+ SNPs: methods methods attempted ld_correct = TRUE, IVW Egger use MendelianRandomization::mr_ivw() MendelianRandomization::mr_egger() correl = TRUE.

title: Perform Mendelian randomisation analysis — run_mr
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Cis-MR using bundled CD40/Sjogren's data bfile <- system.file("extdata", "ld_ref", package = "mrpipeline") result <- run_mr(   exposure = cd40_exposure,   exposure_id = "CD40",   outcome = sjogren_outcome,   outcome_id = "SjD",   instrument_region = list(chromosome = "20", start = 44746911, end = 44758502),   bfile = bfile,   methods = c("ivw", "egger", "weighted_median") ) result summary(result) } # }

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- run_mr(   exposure = cd40_exposure, exposure_id = "CD40",   outcome = sjogren_outcome, outcome_id = "SjD",   instrument_region = list(chromosome = "20", start = 44746911, end = 44758502),   bfile = system.file("extdata", "ld_ref", package = "mrpipeline") ) summary(result) } # }

title: UKBPPP_PQTL_FILE_NAME — ukbppp_pqtl_file_name
text: synapse_id String, synapse id access olink linker file olink_linker_file String Dataframe, file containing olink linker file, dataframe linker file. function designed handle full linker file perform necessary filtering validation internally. can handle cases single synapse_id maps multiple rows (e.g., protein complexes), long underlying file paths consistent. olink_dir String, directory olink files olink_rsid_dir String, directory olink rsid files

title: UKBPPP_PQTL_FILE_NAME — ukbppp_pqtl_file_name
text: list 2 filepaths, one ukbppp_pqtl data corresponding rsID metadata file, well name assay

title: UKBPPP_PQTL_FILE_NAME — ukbppp_pqtl_file_name
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Assuming you have a linker file and the deCODE data directory synapse_id <- "syn12345678" olink_linker_file <- "path/to/your/olink_linker_file.csv" olink_dir <- "path/to/olink_dir" olink_rsid_dir <- "path/to/olink_rsid_dir" file_paths <- ukbppp_pqtl_file_name(synapse_id = synapse_id,                                   olink_linker_file = olink_linker_file,                                   olink_dir = olink_dir,                                   olink_rsid_dir = olink_rsid_dir) print(file_paths) } # }
